notes: Conley & Domingue 2016 (PMC5679002): PGS educational attainment across three HRS birth cohorts. All cohorts in Collapse regime (omega > 0.50). PGS channels are relatively homogeneous (small heterogeneity gap delta ~ 0.01-0.02), meaning the scalar PGS does not compress away much within-level channel heterogeneity. The dominant signal is LOW FIDELITY across all channels (F < 0.50 for all cohorts), corresponding to PGS explaining only ~2.5% of phenotypic variance. The missing heritability (40% twin h-squared vs 2.5% PGS) manifests as high drift omega, not as within-level delta. F declines monotonically across cohorts (0.463 -> 0.410 -> 0.353), falsifying the H&M prediction of increasing genetic stratification.
